title: Contribute Compute
text:
This research project needs Monte Carlo simulation runs across thousands of seeds and four scenario dimensions. You can contribute compute power using GitHub Codespaces — no local setup required.

Open a Codespace macOS Setup View Results

title: Contribute Compute
text:
What We Are Computing

Each simulation run models a refrigerated dog food freight shipment from a distribution facility to a retail store under realistic conditions:

4 scenario combinations: (dry vs refrigerated) x (diesel vs BEV powertrain)
Paired-origin design: centralized (Topeka, KS) vs regionalized (Ennis, TX) distribution
Stochastic traffic: time-of-day speed variation, incident delays, charging queues
Full cold-chain physics: TRU power draw, BEV charging stops, HOS rest breaks

Each worker runs n=200-500 Monte Carlo draws per scenario, producing 1,600-4,000 paired runs per batch. The functional unit is kg CO2e per 1,000 kcal delivered to retail.

title: Contribute Compute
text:
Codespace Quickstart

Time: ~5 minutes setup, then runs autonomously. Cost: Free with GitHub education or included Codespace hours.

Fork this repository to your GitHub account
Click Code > Codespaces > Create codespace on hotfix/derived-bootstrap-fix
Wait for the environment to build (~3-5 minutes)
Open a terminal and run:

N=200 SEED=$((RANDOM + 16000)) bash tools/codespace_run_production.sh
That’s it. The script validates the environment, then runs all 4 scenarios with your unique seed.
For maximum throughput (runs 2 scenarios in parallel):
export R_LIBS_USER="$HOME/.local/share/R/site-library"
mkdir -p sources/data/osm outputs
SEED=$((RANDOM + 16000))
STAMP="contribution_seed${SEED}_$(hostname -s)"

# Worker A: dry scenarios (background)
nohup bash -c "
  for PW in diesel bev; do
    Rscript tools/run_route_sim_mc.R --config test_kit.yaml \
      --scenario ANALYSIS_CORE --product_type dry --powertrain \$PW \
      --paired_origin_networks true --traffic_mode stochastic \
      --n 200 --seed $SEED --artifact_mode summary_only \
      --bundle_root outputs/run_bundle/${STAMP}/dry_\${PW} \
      --summary_out outputs/run_bundle/${STAMP}/dry_\${PW}/summary.csv \
      --runs_out outputs/run_bundle/${STAMP}/dry_\${PW}/runs.csv
  done
" > /tmp/worker_A.log 2>&1 &

# Worker B: refrigerated scenarios (background)
nohup bash -c "
  for PW in diesel bev; do
    Rscript tools/run_route_sim_mc.R --config test_kit.yaml \
      --scenario ANALYSIS_CORE --product_type refrigerated --powertrain \$PW \
      --paired_origin_networks true --traffic_mode stochastic \
      --n 200 --seed $SEED --artifact_mode summary_only \
      --bundle_root outputs/run_bundle/${STAMP}/refrigerated_\${PW} \
      --summary_out outputs/run_bundle/${STAMP}/refrigerated_\${PW}/summary.csv \
      --runs_out outputs/run_bundle/${STAMP}/refrigerated_\${PW}/runs.csv
  done
" > /tmp/worker_B.log 2>&1 &

echo "Running. Monitor: tail -f /tmp/worker_A.log"

title: Contribute Compute
text:
macOS Quickstart

Requirements: macOS with Homebrew (or willingness to install it). ~2 min setup if R is already installed.
git clone --branch hotfix/derived-bootstrap-fix --single-branch \
  https://github.com/dMac716/coldchain-freight-montecarlo.git ~/coldchain-repo
cd ~/coldchain-repo
N=200 SEED=$((RANDOM + 20000)) AUTO_RUN=true bash tools/bootstrap_macos_worker.sh
The script installs R (via Homebrew), required packages, validates the derived data, runs a smoke test, and launches production.
Monitor progress: tail -f /tmp/coldchain_worker.log

title: Contribute Compute
text:
How Results Are Used

Each run produces paired comparisons (centralized vs regionalized distribution) under Common Random Numbers (CRN), enabling statistically fair scenario deltas. Results are aggregated across all contributors to build convergent distributions of:

Transport CO2 emissions per functional unit
Trip duration and driver hours
BEV charging frequency and delay
Diesel refueling patterns
Cold-chain energy burden breakdown

These feed the graduate-level analysis documented in the Methodology page.

title: Contribute Compute
text:
Uploading Results

After your run completes, the results are in outputs/run_bundle/. To share them:
Option 1 — GitHub Issue (simplest): Tar the output and attach to an issue:
cd ~/coldchain-repo  # or /workspaces/coldchain-freight-montecarlo in Codespace
tar czf my_results.tar.gz outputs/run_bundle/
# Attach my_results.tar.gz to a GitHub issue on this repo
Option 2 — GCS upload (if you have gsutil configured):
GCS_BUCKET=coldchain-freight-sources N=200 SEED=20001 \
bash tools/worker_run_and_upload.sh

title: Route and Evolution Animations
text:
Animation assets from local_chunked_run baseline. The production audit (audit_2026-03-17, 70,174 runs) validated these route geometries at scale across 4 GCP and 4 Azure workers.

All videos are linked as direct MP4 files.
If inline playback fails in GitHub Pages, download the animation ZIP.

title: Route and Evolution Animations
text:
Animation assets from local_chunked_run baseline. The production audit (audit_2026-03-17, 70,174 runs) validated these route geometries at scale across 4 GCP and 4 Azure workers.

All videos are linked as direct MP4 files.
If inline playback fails in GitHub Pages, download the animation ZIP.

status in {OK, blank, missing/NA}

matched pair_id enforced (both origins required per pair)
functional_unit_basis = per_1000kcal





candidate valid rows before case selection: 80
rows in selected case before pair matching: 40
rows after matched-pair enforcement: 40
matched pairs used: 20





Graphic 1 metric: co2_per_1000kcal (kg CO2 / 1000 kcal delivered)
Graphic 2 basis: diesel gal / 1000 kcal
Graphic 2 uses propulsion and TRU components normalized per 1000 kcal from run-level values





Paired-comparison logic preserved using pair_id matching.
No cross-scenario/powertrain mixing inside the selected case.
For Graphic 2, direct CO2 propulsion/TRU split was not used when not cleanly populated; direct fuel/energy components were used per FU basis.
Packaging assumptions are representative logistics assumptions (retailer-informed/derived), not exact manufacturer-certified shipping specifications.
Packaging assumptions primarily affect cube utilization and truckload assignment; route energy/emissions physics are still driven by distance, speed/traffic, powertrain, and charging/refueling behavior.

title: Cold-Chain Freight Monte Carlo
text:
Distributed Monte Carlo simulation of greenhouse gas emissions for refrigerated dog food freight under alternative spatial and powertrain scenarios.
Latest production audit: audit_2026-03-17 | 70,174 runs | TRAFFIC_AWARE_OPTIMAL routing Functional unit: kg CO2e / 1,000 kcal delivered to retail

Methodology Route Map Transport Results Contribute Compute

title: Cold-Chain Freight Monte Carlo
text:
Study Design

This simulation compares the lifecycle transport emissions of two distribution strategies for dog food products under diesel and battery-electric (BEV) powertrains:



Dimension
Levels




Spatial
Centralized (Topeka, KS) vs Regionalized (Ennis, TX)


Powertrain
Diesel Cascadia vs BEV eCascadia


Product
Dry (kibble) vs Refrigerated (fresh/frozen)


Traffic
Stochastic (time-of-day variation, incidents)



Each paired draw uses Common Random Numbers (CRN) — exogenous uncertainty is sampled once per seed, then both spatial networks are evaluated with the same draw, enabling statistically fair delta estimation.

title: Cold-Chain Freight Monte Carlo
text:
Latest Results (March 2026 Production Audit)

The production audit aggregated 70,174 deduplicated runs across 8 scenarios (BEV/diesel x dry/refrigerated x centralized/regionalized) from 4 GCP and 4 Azure workers. Key findings:

BEV reduces CO2 85.8% for refrigerated products (cold-chain dominated)
BEV increases CO2 27–32% for dry products (longer BEV routes, charging overhead, grid carbon)
Pre-fix BEV runs had 0 charging stops; post-fix runs correctly model en-route charging




CO2 by scenario





Electrification impact


See the full audit figures on the Methodology and Transport Results pages.

title: Cold-Chain Freight Monte Carlo
text:
Pipeline Overview





flowchart LR
  A[data/inputs_local/*.csv] --> B[R/01_validate.R]
  B --> C[R/sim/* route simulation]
  C --> D[tools/run_route_sim_mc.R]
  D --> E[outputs/run_bundle/]
  E --> F[GCS upload]
  F --> G[Aggregate + analyze]
  G --> H[site/ Quarto pages]
  H --> I[docs/ GitHub Pages]

title: Cold-Chain Freight Monte Carlo
text:
Reproducibility


All pages consume committed artifacts — no live services queried during rendering
Every simulation run records model_version, inputs_hash, RNG seed, and timestamp
Route geometry uses Google Routes API with TRAFFIC_AWARE_OPTIMAL via direct shell curl
Derived data files are committed to the repository for offline reproducibility

Offline-first Artifact-driven Deterministic under fixed seed

title: Cold-Chain Freight Monte Carlo
text:
Quick Start
git clone --branch hotfix/derived-bootstrap-fix \
  https://github.com/dMac716/coldchain-freight-montecarlo.git
cd coldchain-freight-montecarlo
make test          # Run testthat suite
make smoke         # End-to-end smoke test
quarto render site/  # Build this site locally

title: Methodology and Scientific Results
text:
This release documents the production audit audit_2026-03-17 aggregating 70,174 deduplicated runs across 8 scenarios from multi-cloud workers (4 GCP + 4 Azure).

Scope: transport/distribution stage only
Functional unit: kg CO2e / 1000 kcal delivered to retail
Design: matched-pair uncertainty within powertrain
Scenarios: BEV/diesel x dry/refrigerated x centralized/regionalized
Runs: 70,174 (deduplicated)
Validation: PASS

Previous baseline: local_chunked_run (80 runs, 2026-03-09) retained below for reference.

title: Methodology and Scientific Results
text:
This release documents the production audit audit_2026-03-17 aggregating 70,174 deduplicated runs across 8 scenarios from multi-cloud workers (4 GCP + 4 Azure).

Scope: transport/distribution stage only
Functional unit: kg CO2e / 1000 kcal delivered to retail
Design: matched-pair uncertainty within powertrain
Scenarios: BEV/diesel x dry/refrigerated x centralized/regionalized
Runs: 70,174 (deduplicated)
Validation: PASS

Previous baseline: local_chunked_run (80 runs, 2026-03-09) retained below for reference.

title: Methodology and Scientific Results
text:
Study Design

Scenario Structure

dry_diesel: Kansas origin, reefer OFF, diesel powertrain
refrigerated_diesel: Texas origin, reefer ON, diesel powertrain
dry_bev: Kansas origin, reefer OFF, BEV powertrain
refrigerated_bev: Texas origin, reefer ON, BEV powertrain



Paired-Uncertainty Strategy
Within each powertrain, dry/refrigerated rows share operating-condition draws (traffic, departure, ambient, stochastic delay). Paired deltas therefore isolate refrigeration + origin effects under matched operating environments.


Reporting Metric
For each row:

total_kcal_delivered = payload_kg_delivered * energy_density_kcal_per_kg
co2_per_1000kcal = total_trip_co2_kg / total_kcal_delivered * 1000

title: Methodology and Scientific Results
text:
Input Data and Assumptions

Primary Inputs

Route/run outputs from outputs/distribution/local_chunked_run/phase2/*
Functional-unit transformed rows in transport_sim_rows.csv
Scenario and product assumptions from data/inputs_local/*.csv



Packaging Assumptions Used

Dry primary packaging: measured 0.1653 kg per 13.6 kg bag (0.0121 fraction)
Refrigerated primary packaging: measured mean 0.029 kg/package (27–31 g sensitivity)
Secondary refrigerated cardboard: treated as separate unmeasured term (not silently added)

title: Methodology and Scientific Results
text:
Validation and QA Gates
Fail-fast checks enforce:

scenario completeness (4 rows per replicate)
paired-uncertainty consistency within powertrain
dry reefer disabled (tru_kwh=0, tru_gal=0, refrigeration_runtime_hours=0)
route completion before FU export
finite positive total_kcal_delivered
finite co2_per_1000kcal
finite traffic metrics
plausible route distance/time

Validation artifact:

Validation report

title: Methodology and Scientific Results
text:
March 2026 Production Audit (70,174 runs)

The production audit (2026-03-17) aggregated 70,174 deduplicated runs from 4 GCP and 4 Azure workers across all 8 scenario combinations. A critical BEV charging fix was applied mid-campaign: pre-fix runs had 0 charging stops, post-fix runs correctly model en-route DCFC charging events.

Audit Figures

Fig C: Cumulative distribution functions





Fig D: Emissions decomposition (propulsion vs TRU)

Fig H: Electrification impact (BEV vs diesel delta)





Fig I: Driver time components





Fig J: TRU energy by product type




BEV Charging Fix Context
The BEV charging continuation fix (PR #31) resolved three root causes that prevented en-route DCFC charging events from being modeled. Pre-fix BEV runs showed 0 charging stops; post-fix runs correctly model charging stops proportional to route distance.


BEV Charging Validation
The table below compares BEV runs before and after the charging fix, demonstrating that the fix produced the expected non-zero charging stops and associated energy/time impacts.

title: Methodology and Scientific Results
text:
Codespaces Validation Lane Status

Codespaces validation (N_REPS=1) passed with expected paired structure and route identity checks.
Last confirmed chunked production checkpoint from Codespaces logs: chunk_done id=1 reps_done=2/20, then chunk_start id=2.
Additional live status polling was intermittently blocked by GitHub SSH/API instability from this host.

title: Methodology and Scientific Results
text:
Methods Reproduction
# 1) Merge run batches for reporting
Rscript tools/merge_mc_batches.R \
  --bundle_roots outputs/distribution/local_chunked_run/phase2/diesel,outputs/distribution/local_chunked_run/phase2/bev \
  --runs_out outputs/summaries/local_chunked_run_runs_merged.csv \
  --summary_out outputs/summaries/local_chunked_run_summary_merged.csv

# 2) Build presentation/scientific graphics + route animations
RUN_BEV_VALIDATION=false RUN_ADVANCED_DIAGNOSTICS=true RUN_SCIENTIFIC_GRAPHICS=true \
RUN_BEV_GROUPING_DIAGNOSTIC=false RUN_ROUTE_ANIMATION=true \
BUNDLE_ROOT=outputs/distribution/local_chunked_run/phase2 \
OUTDIR=outputs/presentation/transport_graphics_local_chunked_run \
RUNS_CSV=outputs/summaries/local_chunked_run_runs_merged.csv \
ANIM_OUTDIR=docs/assets/transport/animations/local_chunked_run \
bash tools/regenerate_transport_graphics.sh local_chunked_run

# 3) Render site
quarto render site/

title: Route Map: Factory to Retail
text:
Route Details
This map displays the actual Google Routes API driving routes from each distribution facility to the retail destination (Petco, Davis CA). Routes are computed with TRAFFIC_AWARE_OPTIMAL routing preference, providing both traffic-adjusted and static (free-flow) durations.

Blue routes: Topeka, KS (dry product facility) — 1,712 miles
Orange routes: Ennis, TX (refrigerated product facility) — 1,774 miles
Solid lines: Primary (rank 1) route; dashed: alternative routes
Click any route segment for distance, duration, and routing metadata

These routes are the geometric basis for the BEV charging waypoint plans and the Monte Carlo transport simulation.


Route Usage in Production Audit (70,174 runs)

FAF Freight Flow Context
The simulation also draws from USDA Freight Analysis Framework (FAF5) origin-destination flow data to model realistic distance distributions for the centralized and regionalized spatial scenarios.

400 origin-destination pairs from FAF5
Scenarios: CENTRALIZED, REGIONALIZED
Distance range: 25 – 1750 miles
